registry_data.description: Unified SSOT registry combining governance metadata AND Python source code tracking

geu_role_definitions.SCHEMA: Formal definition of allowed structure of an artifact. Registered in schema registry and versioned. Multiple schemas may belong to one GEU.
geu_role_definitions.RULE: Single source of truth (SSOT) definition or policy describing an invariant or lifecycle rule. May include logic or constraints beyond pure schema.
geu_role_definitions.VALIDATOR: Code or configuration enforcing semantic rules beyond schema (e.g., cross-field constraints, uniqueness, registry existence). Validates complete records.
geu_role_definitions.RUNNER: Gate orchestrator that invokes validators, checks schemas, enforces failure modes and ensures deterministic execution.
geu_role_definitions.FAILURE_MODE: Declarative definition of failure classes and actions (block, warn, info). Governs system response to validation failures.
geu_role_definitions.EVIDENCE: Definitions and schemas for logs, diffs, manifests or proof artifacts produced during execution. Ensures verifiable outcomes.
geu_role_definitions.TEST: Automated tests verifying the GEU (unit tests for validators, integration tests for runners).
geu_role_definitions.REPORT: Scripts or templates producing human-readable reports from evidence. Optional for many GEUs.

schema_based.description: Typical for data models or contract schemas. Schema is primary anchor defining allowed structure.

rule_based.description: Used when canonical source is a policy or rule (e.g., lifecycle policy). Rule may include multiple schemas.

runner_based.description: Enforcement anchored by executable gate (e.g., CI runner orchestrating multiple rules). Schema or rule should exist.

data_transformation.description: Enforces data transformations. Both input and output schemas must be present to validate transformation results.
data_transformation.notes: Requires both input and output schemas

evidence_only.description: Primary function is to collect and bundle evidence (e.g., audit report). Needs minimal runner and failure mode.
